This mission requires that you find a city location (a type of vertical location card that notes the name of the city/town/village on the card) and perform multiple overpower[/b] actions there. Gain the Map of Cities (1251[/b]).The capital city is not eligible to be conquered, as it is too big.

1593.action: RESEARCH[/b] how you can complete the mission (this action can be performed at most once per game)
1593.text: You are here on behalf of a cohort of ambassadors seeking alliances with new worlds. The best way to form strong bonds is to prove your worth to the cultures on this planet. Gain the Map of Trials (1261[/b]).

1594.action: RESEARCH[/b] how you can complete the mission (this action can be performed at most once per game)
1594.text: You work for a mega-corporation, Calligraph Corp, and you’ve been tasked with locating and gathering precious minerals from various planets. Your ship’s sensors indicated that powerful crystals are abundant in this world, some in constant motion in the bodies of animals, others remaining in one place under skeletons. Gain the Map of Skeletons (1254[/b]).

1595.action: RESEARCH[/b] how you can complete the mission (this action can be performed at most once per game)
1595.text: A dangerous dictator rules your homeworld with an iron fist, and you managed to escape the blockade there to seek allies, weapons, and skills. Any card with an overpower[/b] slot—even a specific ability— counts toward this goal. Some of these items are found in treasure chests. Gain the Map of Treasure (1260[/b]).

1596.action: RESEARCH[/b] how you can complete the mission (this action can be performed at most once per game)
1596.text: This planet appeared on your radar because of a signal received from the mysterious Traveler. Finding the source of the Traveler’s signal is key, and its encryption seems similar to that used by your ship’s own comm systems. The closest your incoming ship was able to pinpoint the signal was somewhere in the middle of the southeastern region of the world (view page 5 of the Book of Vantages).

1597.action: RESEARCH[/b] how you can complete the mission (this action can be performed at most once per game)
1597.text: You swore to your family that your heisting days will be behind you… if you complete one last job with the help of your old team. While there are plenty of things to steal on this planet, a heist isn’t petty theft: You can offer your help to sentients to gain and complete a heist quest, or you can earn a precious artifact by passing a test. Gain the Map of Artifacts (1264[/b]).

1598.action: RESEARCH[/b] how you can complete the destiny (this action can be performed at most once per game)
1598.text: When combined, the towers on this planet are advanced enough to transmit a powerful signal. Gain the Map of Towers (1259[/b]), and each player gains 1 engage[/b].

1599.action: RESEARCH[/b] how you can complete the destiny (this action can be performed at most once per game)
1599.text: Communication is crucially important in trying to track down your crewmates. Climbing towers to get a view from above may also help. Your cats love trees, so you’re most likely to find them in the forest canopy. Each player gains 1 random skill.

160.text: A pair of burly sentients is guarding the entrance from the shadows. Inside the building, you can hear a constant clanking and the whistle of bellows—you’re pretty sure this is a forge. Gain 2 boost and 1 move[/b]; you may continue.

1600.action: RESEARCH[/b] how you can complete the destiny (this action can be performed at most once per game)
1600.text: There are quite a few houses scattered across the world, but they are occupied by sentients—you could save up enough money to buy one of them. Or you could seek opportunities to build your own structure and make it a liveable space. Gain $5, and each player gains 1 help[/b].

1601.action: RESEARCH[/b] how you can complete the destiny (this action can be performed at most once per game)
1601.text: In such a vast world, there are quite a few patches of land where you might build a business. But you could also seek out employment in cities, taking over an existing shop. Gain the Map of Cities (1251[/b]), and each player gains 1 help[/b].

1602.action: RESEARCH[/b] how you can complete the destiny (this action can be performed at most once per game)
1602.text: You hear rumors of a dragon living in a mountain just north of the windswept grasslands. You also learn that not all elder dragons are old—legend tells of a special egg that, when hatched, will bring a new elder dragon into this world. Gain the Map of Caves (1252[/b]), and each player gains 1 random skill.

1603.action: RESEARCH[/b] how you can complete the destiny (this action can be performed at most once per game)
1603.text: On this world, it seems that no greater glory can be achieved than in the card and dice game, Skirm. Seek out duels whenever you can, and try your luck in opening treasure chests to find more Skirm cards. Gain the Map of Treasure (1260[/b]), and each player gains 1 overpower[/b].

1604.action: RESEARCH[/b] how you can complete the destiny (this action can be performed at most once per game)
1604.text: Teleportation blurs the line between science and magic; in this world, using a spell to bridge great distances isn’t unheard of. Gain the Book of Spells for Spirit magic (1214[/b]), and each player gains 1 move[/b].

1605.action: RESEARCH[/b] how you can complete the destiny (this action can be performed at most once per game)
1605.text: Your spaceship shattered into several pieces across the planet during the crash. Be prepared for an extensive repair process. Gain 2 help[/b]; each other player gains 1 help[/b].

1606.action: RESEARCH[/b] how you can complete the destiny (this action can be performed at most once per game)
1606.text: The people of this world traverse the planet in a variety of ways, and almost any vehicle can be upgraded into something better. If you repeat this process enough times, you’ll find yourself in a motley but fully functional spaceship. Gain 2 help[/b]; each other player gains 1 help[/b].

1607.action: RESEARCH[/b] how you can complete the destiny (this action can be performed at most once per game)
1607.text: The spaceship that brought you to this planet was a modern marvel of technological wizardry. It would take dozens of years to rebuild it from scratch. Fortunately, there are some time anomalies on this planet that may reduce the time you need to have a ship built. You may need to hire someone to oversee the job. Gain $5, and each player gains 1 engage[/b].

1608.action: RESEARCH[/b] how you can complete the destiny (this action can be performed at most once per game)
1608.text: Time travel may be a fictional concept where (and when) you come from, but some sentients of this planet shift through the sands of time on a daily basis. Learn from their wisdom to undo this terrible crash. Gain the Book of Spells for Mind magic (1215[/b]), and each player gains 1 look[/b].

161.text: The sentient explains that you’re both standing on their mother, a fledgling volcano, and their duty is to release the lava so that the volcano can expand and flourish. It seems like tiring work, so you could violently smash the rock along with them. Gain 1 boost and continue.

162.action: SEARCH[/b] for a back-alley entrance after crossing the inlet
162.text: A door on the eastern side of the city is propped open to release the intense heat. You hear the clang of metal inside. If you enter, gain 1 look[/b], go to city 525[/b], and continue with the look-NAVIGATE[/b][/b] action there. Otherwise, go to city 525[/b].

163.text: The beast rocks back and forth as if hoping you’ll play with it or give it a treat. Gain 1 boost and either 1 engage[/b] or 1 help[/b]; you may continue.

164.action: PREPARE[/b] to sneak over to the eye
164.text: You perceptively notice a large figure lurking below the surface of the lava. Choose one: Gain 1 random skill and 1 skill of your choice, refresh all challenge dice from a row of your grid, or gain 1 boost on each card in a column of your grid. Continue.

1645.action: INSPECT[/b] the scene of the crime
1645.text: The victim is wrapped in sailcloth, and their partner hesitantly allows you to take a closer look, saying that no one has seen the body but them. They haven’t discussed it with anyone. Despite the volume of blood on the floorboards, the wound is narrow and shallow, about the length of your forearm—a clean cut.

165.text: You find a tight gap in the wreckage that you might be able to squeeze through. It’s surprising that, despite the plummet from outer space, this part of the ship seems operational. Gain 1 boost and destiny 1605[/b] (place it in the center); you may continue.

166.action: SPY[/b] on the house from a safe distance
166.text: You can see shadows moving around the house, but before you move any closer, you notice the pustules on the tree near you are ready to burst. It’s probably safest to greet the occupants from your current position. Gain 1 boost and 1 engage[/b]; you may continue.

167.text: The sentient is too exhausted to explain much, but they share the basics of smelting with you and warn you of the elemental to the east. Gain ability 1475[/b] and 1 boost.

168.action: PREPARE[/b] to interact with the elemental
168.text: Choose one: Gain 1 random skill and 1 skill of your choice, refresh all challenge dice from a row of your grid, or gain 1 boost on each card in a column of your grid. Then continue with either the help-RELEASE[/b][/b] action or the overpower-SLAY[/b][/b] action.

1680.action: SCAN[/b] the bridge for sneaky intruders
1680.text: In the dim lantern light, every shadow is a suspect. Your eyes grow heavy as the night wears on, but you’re alert enough to catch several street rats shimmying along the side of the bridge. Add 1 token to quest 1680[/b].

1687.action: REFLECT[/b] on the joys of home ownership
1687.text: You sip contentedly on some tea as you appreciate the view. Gain destiny 1600[/b] (place it in the center; you have completed it and may end the game when you’re ready) and 1 boost on your character.

1688.action: REFLECT[/b] on the joys of home ownership
1688.text: You sip contentedly on some tea as you appreciate the view. Gain destiny 1600[/b] (place it in the center; you have completed it and may end the game when you’re ready) and 1 boost on your character.

1689.action: REFLECT[/b] on the joys of home ownership
1689.text: You sip contentedly on some tea as you appreciate the view. Gain destiny 1600[/b] (place it in the center; you have completed it and may end the game when you’re ready) and 1 boost on your character.

169.text: You can’t miss the massive elemental to the west of here, entrapped within a barrier nearly identical to the one before you. The Traveler explains that long ago, a fire wizard invented a way to forge new elementals. Their people decided to destroy this knowledge and never use it, as it would cause the end of the world. Gain 2 boost and continue.

1690.action: REFLECT[/b] on the joys of home ownership
1690.text: You sip contentedly on some tea as you appreciate the view. Gain destiny 1600[/b] (place it in the center; you have completed it and may end the game when you’re ready) and 1 boost on your character.

1691.action: REFLECT[/b] on the joys of home ownership
1691.text: You sip contentedly on some tea as you appreciate the view. Gain destiny 1600[/b] (place it in the center; you have completed it and may end the game when you’re ready) and 1 boost on your character.

1692.action: REFLECT[/b] on the joys of home ownership
1692.text: You sip contentedly on some tea as you appreciate the view. Gain destiny 1600[/b] (place it in the center; you have completed it and may end the game when you’re ready) and 1 boost on your character.

170.action: EXAMINE[/b] the rock and the arch
170.text: The sight is so completely foreign that you don’t know what the structure does, but you do recognize that the stone would fit perfectly in the concavity on the left side of the arch. Choose one:

choices.outcome: Gain 1 boost and continue.

choices.option: Pay 2 boost or 1 move to push the rock into the concavity
choices.outcome: The rock slides seamlessly into place. As it does, a jolt surges up your spine and down your arms. You’ve been endowed with a new power. Gain spell 1367[/b].

1701.action: REFLECT[/b] on the joys of home ownership
1701.text: You sip contentedly on some tea as you appreciate the view. Gain destiny 1600[/b] (place it in the center; you have completed it and may end the game when you’re ready) and 1 boost on your character.

1705.action: NAVIGATE[/b] the rescue vessel toward your location, ending your adventure
1705.text: The game ends as you head back to your homeworld, and you achieve an epic victory if you have also completed the mission. Otherwise, it is impressive that you survived long enough to control your destiny, and your knowledge of the planet will benefit you in the future.

171.text: You avoid the lava while inspecting the orb. The orb is made of a shimmering, glass-like substance, and there are markings on the outside with which you might be able to interact. You don’t see how to access the interior, but there must be a way inside. Gain 1 boost and either 1 engage[/b] or 1 take[/b]; you may continue.

172.action: NAVIGATE[/b] the boat away from the volcano
172.text: None of the leaf sentients on the ship are at the helm when you drag yourself onto the deck, so you take over while they compliment your navigation skills. Choose one:

choices.option: Guide the boat to the northwest shoreline
choices.outcome: In gratitude, the sentients put you on their shoulders and carry you over the ridge. During the trek, they discuss plans for their quest to free some enslaved miners to the north. Gain 1 time and quest 1671[/b] (add it horizontally to your supply), then go to 252[/b].

choices.option: Guide the boat to the east of the volcano
choices.outcome: The sentients grumble that you’ve steered them away from their destination to the northwest, but they’re happy to be alive. Gain 1 morale and $3, then go to 173[/b].

173.text: Each player—including you—on a location with any rune (a skill icon within their vantage) gains 1 boost. Then you roll X skill dice and gain all results matching the rune on this location. Also gain quest 1706[/b] if a player doesn’t already have it (add it horizontally to your supply and place the first token on it).

174.text: Your impressive feat of memory impresses the fire sentients, who welcome you to participate in the trial. Continue with the take-ENDURE[/b][/b] action.

175.text: You take a few steps back, which seems to assure the sentient that you don’t want any trouble. A whiff of the sparkling substance somehow both enhances and numbs your senses at the same time, and you’re simultaneously aware of some dancing sentients to the east, a large animal in the south, and an elemental monster to the west. Lose 1 health and gain 3 boost; you may continue.

176.action: OBSERVE[/b] the sentients as they flail in the wind
176.text: You’re worried at first, but then your concerns turn to laughter as you realize the sentients are dancing around the twister! They might even be creating the funnel of dust with their wings. There is a pattern to their dance, and you quickly memorize it. Gain ability 1469[/b]; you may continue.

177.text: You notice that there are a number of winged sentients in the area, but they all steer clear of this particular patch of land. It is probably sacred to them, and you’re pleased with yourself for figuring that out. Gain 1 morale and 1 boost; you may continue.

178.text: You pretend not to notice the sentient while you inspect the flowers with care. They’re beautiful, and they give you a sense of appreciation for the life around you, but there is also something sad about them. Gain quest 1629[/b] (add it horizontally to your supply).

179.text: The sentient is completely absorbed by the flowers, touching them, taking samples, even whispering to them. It’s endearing to see someone so entranced by nature. The smell of the flowers drifts over to you while you watch; the odor has a hint of something you can only describe as empathy or compassion, and you feel more connected to the sentients around you. Gain 1 morale and 1 boost.

180.text: The elder is a slow but eager teacher. They talk about the wind elemental to the west, a massive, ancient creature that is as revered as it is feared. Only an outsider would dare confront it. Gain 1 boost and 1 overpower[/b].

181.text: The sentients don’t say a word or acknowledge you, but you can feel tension in the air as you stand nearby. Something very important to them is in that chest. You’re surrounded in almost every direction by similar sentients going about their day in this prairie region, and the sounds of a city come from the north. Gain 1 boost and continue.

182.text: The sentient flexes as they help you to your feet, clearly pleased to have the upper hand. Pointing to the giant horn to the north, they explain that they’re just doing their job to protect the artifact. With a wink, they suggest that the first note is already in the correct position. Gain 2 boost.

183.text: At first it’s difficult to discern the sentient’s dance moves, but a distinct pattern emerges and you’re able to memorize it. Gain ability 1469[/b].

184.text: Your impressive feat of memory impresses the wind queen, who welcomes you to participate in the trial. Continue with the help-ENLIVEN[/b][/b] action.

185.action: SCAN[/b] the ruins for anything useful
185.text: You identify a small snare among the rubble. Gain 1 boost and 1 look[/b]; go to interior 542[/b].

186.text: You follow sweetly scented smoke drifting from an open door. If you enter, gain 2 take[/b], go to city 518[/b], and continue with the take-ACCESS[/b][/b] action there. Otherwise, go to city 518[/b].

187.text: Find card 1140[/b] and place it on the table. You must demonstrate that you know the harmony of the winds by playing the sacred notes on the horn. Discern those notes based on the four previous performers, whose attempts are judged with feathers. The blue feathers represent how many times a correct note was played in the correct place in the melody. The red feathers represent how many times a correct note was played but in the wrong place. All other notes are incorrect. Proceed to study the previous attempts before announcing the four notes you will perform (from among A-G; order matters). See entry 187[/b] in the Book of Secrets for the solution.

188.text: The old wind sentient, clearly distraught by the broken turbine, paces in front of you. “This has been in my family for generations. Sure, it’s a piece of junk now, but it was one of the first windmills of any kind in these parts. I feel like I’ve let my family down and that it’s too late to fix it.” Seeing that you’re in no rush and that you’re really listening, they stop pacing and start sharing fond memories of their childhood on this little patch of land. Gain ability 1494[/b] and 1 morale; you may continue.

189.action: NAVIGATE[/b] the optimal path across the wetlands
189.text: As you listen to a rusty turbine creaking to the west, you find a way to leap from one sandbar to the next. Gain ability 1486[/b] and 1 boost; you may continue.

019.action: SOLVE[/b] the glyph puzzle on the door
019.text: Your mind sometimes surprises you, and you’re able to trace the glyphs in the correct order. The door slides open. Gain 1 boost and go deeper into the planet to cave 046[/b].

190.action: EXPLORE[/b] the mouth of the cave
190.text: As your keen eyes adjust to the darkness, you reach a barrier that requires further attention. Go to cave 036[/b].

191.text: Each player—including you—on a location with any rune (a skill icon within their vantage) gains 1 boost. Then you roll X skill dice and gain all results matching the rune on this location. Also gain quest 1706[/b] if a player doesn’t already have it (add it horizontally to your supply and place the first token on it).

192.action: SPY[/b] on the sentient to the north
192.text: The sentient is in the process of placing more flags into the ground, pausing to check their calculations before each placement. Gain 2 boost and continue.

193.text: “Sometimes I miss my childhood home, but I’ve called this planet my home for far too long to use that word reasonably for anywhere else. If anything, I miss feeling the grass beneath my feet or the wind in my hair.” Gain 1 boost and continue.

194.action: OBSERVE[/b] the scene from a safe distance
194.text: At first you’re unsure what to make of it, but you soon realize that the two sentients are dueling with each other with immense power—they must be using some form of magic—and the tornado is simply a byproduct of the clash. Gain 1 boost and 1 engage[/b]; you may continue.

195.text: The smell of a campfire leads you to a perch among the rocks. There you can see a campsite—a fire and two tents—but no campers. Gain 2 boost and continue.

196.text: You find a tight gap in the wreckage that you might be able to squeeze through. It’s surprising that, despite the plummet from outer space, this part of the ship seems operational. The Traveler peeks inside and says, “You know, with enough effort you could repair the spaceship and return home.” Gain 2 boost and destiny 1605[/b] (place it in the center); you may continue.

197.text: The sentient hesitates as you take in the surrounding area. They don’t seem like a threat—rather, you’re pretty sure they just want to place that flag in the right place in the ground. To the west you can see some remnants of your spaceship, and to the east an orb rests precariously between two foothills. Gain 2 boost and continue.

198.text: The orb is made of a shimmering, glass-like substance, and there are markings on the outside with which you might be able to interact. You don’t see how to access the interior, but there must be a way inside. Gain 1 boost and either 1 engage[/b] or 1 take[/b]; you may continue.

199.text: The sentient is quite proud of their trade, speaking of it as an art form. They even offer you an apprenticeship. Gain sentient 1338[/b] and ability 1471[/b].

020.text: After close examination, you determine that there is no option other than to swap out the magnetic rocks with something of similar size and weight, and you scour the cave entrance for the perfect object. Gain 1 take[/b] and continue.

200.text: You can’t see anything above as you navigate through the thick clouds, but using clues on the surface you manage to find a route to a structure perched on a cliff. Go to interior 563[/b] and gain 1 morale.

201.text: The sentient is patrolling a perimeter, but their attention is to the northeast and they don’t notice you. You notice another sentient working with a device to the east and a similar device to the south. Gain 2 boost and 1 engage[/b]; you may continue.

202.text: In addition to the menacing-yet-friendly sentient and the technology they were working on, you see several patches of flowers. The sentient sees you admiring them and explains that they’re called dawnflare, and that certain animals are quite fond of them. Gain ability 1497[/b] and 2 boost; you may continue.

203.text: From their stance and the motion of their eyes, you have the feeling that they’re guarding something—or someone— important inside the building. They might be susceptible to bribery, or you could probably take them in a fight. Gain 1 boost and either 1 engage[/b] or 1 overpower[/b]. You may continue.

204.action: SPY[/b] on the house through a window
204.text: You’re excited to see a nice wok inside the house; gain 1 boost. Unless you’ve already determined whether the house is occupied, roll a challenge die. If the result is blank, no one is home. If the result is not blank, someone is home. When you finish your reconnaissance, the Traveler appears at your shoulder. “You know, you could save up money and buy a house like this.” Gain destiny 1600[/b] (place it in the center) and continue.

205.text: The lotus smells a bit like coconut. Gain 1 health from the purifying pollen; you may also lose 1 curse.

206.text: You shimmy along the edge of the cliff until you find an open window. Peering inside, you see what appears to be a pawn shop. If you enter, gain 2 engage[/b], go to city 522[/b], and continue with the engage-TRADE[/b][/b] action there. Otherwise, go to city 522[/b].

207.text: Your impressive feat of memory impresses the metal sentient, who welcomes you to participate in the trial. Continue with the take-BYPASS[/b][/b] action.

208.text: The orb is made of a shimmering, glass-like substance, and there are markings on the outside with which you might be able to interact. You don’t see how to access the interior, but there must be a way inside. Gain 1 boost and either 1 engage[/b] or 1 take[/b]; you may continue.

209.text: The animal appears to be looking for a fight, and you could escape by leaping south onto some floating islands or veering east to tropical waters. Gain 2 boost and continue.

021.text: After close examination, you determine that there is no option other than to leap, and you calculate the perfect angle for an attempt. Before you do, you may pay 1 take[/b] or 2 boost to carefully unplug a device near the edge (gain item 988[/b]). Gain 1 move[/b] and continue.

210.action: EXAMINE[/b] the object at close range
210.text: Despite blending into the surrounding stones, it’s a solid block of metal. As you approach, you enter its magnetic field, and with a jolt you’re pulled toward the object, crashing hard and sticking to it. Lose 1 health. You can wrest free by paying 1 move[/b] or 1 overpower[/b]; if you do, go north to 215[/b]. Otherwise, you are stuck and must continue with the move-EVADE[/b][/b] or overpower-CLEAVE[/b][/b] action.

211.text: At first you assume the brilliant orange glow is the sentient themselves, but you realize it’s actually coming from an item they’re trying to hide. You might be able to steal it, or you could avoid creating a scene and just sneak past the sentient under the bridge. Gain 1 boost and either 1 move[/b] or 1 take[/b]; you may continue.

212.text: You remain perfectly still, watching the animal as it watches you. As big as it is, there’s something playful about the beast. You also notice a large lotus behind the animal. Gain 2 boost and 1 engage[/b]; you may continue.

213.text: Several guards patrol the area. The light emanating from the building is so bright that you can’t make out its contents, but it does illuminate a runecaster near the rear of the ruins. Gain 2 boost and 1 random skill; you may continue.

214.action: EXPLORE[/b] the mouth of the cave
214.text: After crawling over the ledge, you enter the cave. As your eyes adjust to the darkness, you reach a barrier that requires further attention. Go to cave 034[/b].

215.text: The tavern is full of half-drunk (and fully drunk) sentients. You could have a drink with them or sneak into a side room. The device hovering near the building also intrigues you. Gain 2 boost and 1 random skill; you may continue.

216.text: You walk past the flowers to join a small tour group gathering near the entrance. One of them tosses a pebble at a gap in the fence, and it’s instantly repelled by a sizzling force field. A security officer guards the front door, but you blend into the group as they enter the museum. Gain 1 boost and go to interior 564[/b].

217.text: Your keen eyes notice that despite the falling snow, there isn’t a flake of snow on any of the chain links—this must be an electric fence. You could disarm the electricity or find another way up and over the fence: Gain 2 boost and continue.

218.action: STUDY[/b] the properties of the magnetic rock
218.text: You can see why there is an aura of mystery and power around this place. As you examine the stone, you realize that your feet are no longer touching the ground—you are levitating in orbit around the stone with the other shards. You kick away, falling to the snow, a newfound power surging through you. Gain lesson 1431[/b].

219.text: The sentient’s eyes never leave the horizon, scanning for non-existent threats while they take you under their tutelage. They explain that their job requires a combination of observation (ability 1477[/b]) and defense (ability 1478[/b])—gain one of those abilities.

022.text: After close examination, you determine that there is no option other than to modify the laser array, and you calculate the perfect angle to redirect the beams. Gain 1 help[/b] and continue.

220.text: You feel a surge of strength as you study the stone and the rune etched into it. For a brief moment you see a vision from the past—a massive elemental using this shard as a weapon against an army of circuit sentients. Gain 1 boost.

221.text: The swift, aggressive animal appears to be guarding the cave. It calms when seeing you consider a more circuitous route. Gain 2 boost and continue.

222.text: It’s difficult to see through the snowstorm, but you hear the sounds of bleating to the far northeast. As scared as the young animal is, you can tell it’s built for fighting, so you may want to gain its trust with kindness. Gain 1 boost and either 1 help[/b] or 1 overpower[/b]; you may continue.

223.text: You feel a surge of wisdom as you study the stone and the rune etched into it. For a brief moment you see a vision from the past—this area was the scene of a truce between the circuit and metal sentients. Gain 1 boost.

224.text: The areas to the north and west are snowy, but a break in the ridge to the east could take you toward a warmer area. The lumpy figures in the distance seem to be moving slowly across the valley—perhaps a herd of animals? Gain 2 boost and continue.

225.action: SPY[/b] through an open seam in the bigger tent, waiting until its occupants take a stroll
225.text: Your eyes light up when you see a shovel inside. Gain 1 boost and 1 take[/b]; go to interior 551[/b].

226.text: At first you think some sort of intense bonfire was creating the beam of light, but the more you watch it, the more you’re convinced there is some sort of glowing item at the source. Gain 2 boost and continue.

227.text: It’s a little different from what you pictured from the books you read as an imaginative child, but there’s no mistaking a flying carpet. The device is touch-sensitive, and it counterbalances against your weight. Looking around, you can’t see its owner. Gain ability 1490[/b]. If you pay 1 boost, also gain vehicle 985[/b]; if not, continue.

228.text: You wave to the sentient as you walk to the edge of the cliff. It’s a long, steep descent to the bottom, and the cliff extends to the east and west—not too far to the east, though. You could probably even climb the nearby tower to get a better view of the swamp below. Perhaps you could enlist the sentient to show you around? Gain ability 1497[/b] and 1 morale, and gain either 1 move[/b] or 1 take[/b]. You may continue.

229.text: Other than Moonstone Mountain itself—which you can imagine that sentients in the valley below might be looking for—you don’t see an end to the cliff in either direction. There is, however, a tower directly to the west that might help. Gain 1 boost and continue.

023.text: After close examination, you determine that there is no option other than to destroy the gate, and you calculate the perfect angle for an attempt. Gain 1 overpower[/b] and continue.

230.text: Each player—including you—on a location with any rune (a skill icon within their vantage) gains 1 boost. Then you roll X skill dice and gain all results matching the rune on this location. Also gain quest 1706[/b] if a player doesn’t already have it (add it horizontally to your supply and place the first token on it).

231.text: Find cards 1137[/b]–1139[/b] and place them on the table. As you do, the air around you is filled with different types of energy: thermal, electric, and chemical. To wrest the gauntlet away from its anchor, you must fully understand how the three types of energy fit together. Assemble the cards so they form 3 digits, then speak those digits in ascending order. See entry 231[/b] in the Book of Secrets for the solution.

232.text: The longer you watch, the more the light begins to resemble an intelligent life form. You realize you’re witnessing the birth of a sentient made of pure energy. The light is the same color as the lanterns that hang all around you—they must be where this type of sentient lives. Gain 1 morale and 1 boost.

233.action: TRANSLATE[/b] a cipher for the judges (only if you have been welcomed as a participant in this trial; otherwise choose another action)
233.text: Find cards 1147[/b]–1149[/b] and place them on the table. An energy sentient points to the lanterns surrounding you on this location (cards 1147[/b] and 1148[/b]), each of which is etched with images of sparks, fire, rocks, mountains, blood, and water in various quantities. The sentient also hands you a scroll with four runes written on it (card 1149[/b]). Your goal is to translate the scroll, which describes a universal mantra involving two numbers and two words. See entry 233[/b] in the Book of Secrets for the solution.

234.text: The trio isn’t particularly eager to teach anything to a complete stranger, but they can’t resist the appeal of learning a new language. After you share a few words with them, they teach you some basic spirit magic and recommend that you stay away from the deadly plants in this area of the swamp. Gain lesson 1424[/b], 1 boost, and 1 look[/b].

235.action: LEARN[/b] about the environment from the Traveler
235.text: “Where we’re standing right now is one of the few places where you could dig, if that’s something you’re into,” the Traveler says. Gain 1 boost and continue.

236.text: The tower has a number of good footholds and handholds; you could get a great view of the surrounding area if you scaled it or use the tower’s transmission capabilities to contact your homeworld. As you study it, one of the lights on the tower changes to a pink hue, and a head begins to emerge from it. Gain 1 boost and any 1 skill. Continue.

237.text: The sentient is eager to practice your language, though they seem a bit nervous. “I don’t like to be this close to the elemental,” they say. “These lanterns are offerings—tiny pieces of our souls—to the energy elemental to the north. We fear it as much as we revere it.” They teach you a bit of spirit magic so you can experience what it feels like to draw energy from your soul. Gain lesson 1425[/b] and 1 morale.

238.text: You find yourself in a busy room with pink sentients rushing in and out. If you enter, gain 2 move[/b], go to city 520[/b], and continue with the move-ENTER[/b][/b] action there. Otherwise, go to city 520[/b].

239.text: Several sentients are admiring a beautiful array of fancy lanterns. You also notice a mysterious green elixir. Gain 1 boost and 1 move[/b]; you may continue.

024.text: The barrier is a pitch-black, endless void. Finally you emerge much deeper into the planet; go to cave 084[/b].

240.text: The orb is made of a shimmering, glass-like substance, and there are markings on the outside with which you might be able to interact. You don’t see how to access the interior, but there must be a way inside. Gain 1 boost and either 1 engage[/b] or 1 take[/b]; you may continue.

241.text: As far as you can tell, this stone acts as a warning for the monstrous elemental to the east. Gain 1 boost and continue.

242.action: PREPARE[/b] to interact with the elemental
242.text: Choose one: Gain 1 random skill and 1 skill of your choice, refresh all challenge dice from a row of your grid, or gain 1 boost on each card in a column of your grid. Then continue with either the help-RELEASE[/b][/b] action or the overpower-SLAY[/b][/b] action.

243.text: Each player—including you—on a location with any rune (a skill icon within their vantage) gains 1 boost. Then you roll X skill dice and gain all results matching the rune on this location. Also gain quest 1706[/b] if a player doesn’t already have it (add it horizontally to your supply and place the first token on it).

244.text: The orb is made of a shimmering, glass-like substance, and there are markings on the outside with which you might be able to interact. You don’t see how to access the interior, but there must be a way inside. Gain 1 boost and either 1 engage[/b] or 1 take[/b]; you may continue.

245.text: Your augsuit goes haywire as you step in the water around the flower. Disoriented, you stumble backwards. Lose 1 morale and gain 1 boost; you may continue.

246.text: Each player—including you—on a location with any rune (a skill icon within their vantage) gains 1 boost. Then you roll X skill dice and gain all results matching the rune on this location. Also gain quest 1706[/b] if a player doesn’t already have it (add it horizontally to your supply and place the first token on it).

247.text: The heated discussion is difficult to comprehend fully, but you gather that these three sentients represent major factions. On the left is someone who believes it is the duty of energy sentients to offer their energy to other sentients. On the right is someone who urges other energy sentients to exact revenge on those who sap their energy. In the middle is someone content to simply live out their life in the swamp. Gain ability 1494[/b] and 3 boost; you may continue.

248.action: TRANSLATE[/b] the markings on the tree
248.text: As you read the runes, they fill your field of vision. Something magical enters your comprehension as if you’ve always known it. Gain lesson 1419[/b].

249.action: OBSERVE[/b] the animal while it observes you
249.text: The staring contest ends with you blinking, which delights the animal. Gain animal 823[/b] and 1 boost.

025.text: After close examination, you determine that there is no option other than to destroy the gate, and you calculate the perfect angle for an attempt. Gain 1 overpower[/b] and continue.

250.text: Despite the cool breeze from the west, you can tell how hot the area to the east and northeast is. You’re curious whether the temperatures bother the sentients walking in the distance. As for more immediate matters, there might be a way to reach the pink box on the ledge, but you risk pricking yourself on the rocky thorns. Gain 2 boost and continue.

251.text: Your keen eyes notice two very important details. Firstly, the hole in the ground isn’t a hole at all—it’s a giant, gaping mouth. Secondly, wedged between two of those teeth is a shiny object, some sort of jewelry. Gain 2 boost, then you may choose one or neither:

choices.option: move--DESCEND into the pit toward the jewelry
choices.outcome: The ground lurches beneath you, and all goes black. Go to cave 049[/b].
Without leaving your perch on the outer rim, you delicately remove the jewelry. Gain item 991[/b].

252.action: OBSERVE[/b] the sentient on their roost
252.text: The sentient stares back at you for a moment but doesn’t seem to consider you a threat, so they turn back to their work, which seems to involve the airborne animals. You also notice the gaping mouth of a sand centipede to the west. Gain 1 boost and 1 random skill. If you pay 1 engage[/b], also gain sentient 1331[/b]; if you don’t pay, you may continue.

253.text: The treasure chest is sturdy (but eroded by sand), with a solid lock and ornate gold trim. Gain 2 boost and continue.

254.text: You realize that it’s not one but two animals that created these footprints. You can tell from the tracks that the animal to the north is hungry and that the animal to the east is scared. Choose one:Gain 1 boost and go north to 259[/b].Gain 1 boost and go east to 443[/b].Gain 1 random skill and continue.

255.text: The sentients immediately back away—they were just excited to meet you. They explain that they’re ready to go on a hunt, but they always hunt as a trio, not a duo. Gain sentient 1333[/b], 1 boost, and quest 1649[/b] (add the quest horizontally to your supply).

256.text: It looks like the beast died from sunstroke. There is a stillness to the air around the skeleton, and you can’t decide whether to use the bones to your advantage or delve underneath. Gain 2 boost and continue.

257.text: It soon becomes clear that the leaf sentient has stumbled into a trap made of bones. Without your help they will succumb to the elements, to the beasts, or to whoever laid the trap. The question is: Do they deserve to be saved? Gain 1 boost and 1 random skill; you may continue.

258.action: LISTEN[/b] to the peddler’s sales pitch
258.text: The sentient complains of the cooler temperatures to the southeast. They carry an amazing array of bone-crafted items, including a $3 pair of climbing shoes (item 963[/b]), a $4 mace (item 969[/b]), a $6 lyre (item 970[/b]), and an $8 sword (item 888[/b]). You may either buy one of them (gain the card) or gain $1.

259.text: The animal acknowledges you with a slight bow before ambling toward a sentient to the west; perhaps it has a kind heart. Gain 1 morale and choose one:

choices.outcome: Gain either 1 engage[/b] or 1 help[/b], then continue.

Your eyes are also drawn to the bottom rune on the rocky column. Each player—including you—on a location with any rune (a skill icon within their vantage) gains 1 boost. Then you roll 4 skill dice and gain all results matching the rune on this location. Also gain quest 1706[/b] if a player doesn’t already have it (add it horizontally to your supply and place the first token on it).

choices.option: Pay 1 engage and place your hand on a rune
choices.outcome: A hidden door opens in the base of the stone spire, revealing a staircase leading into a massive underground cavern. Go to cave 099[/b].

choices.option: Pay 1 overpower to pluck a spiky desert flora
choices.outcome: Gain flora 1053[/b].

026.text: After close examination, you determine that there is no option other than to swap out the widgets with something of similar size and weight, and you scour the cave entrance for the perfect object. Gain 1 take[/b] and continue.

260.action: SCAN[/b] the area for any available skeletons
260.text: You’re happy to see that there are several large, sunbaked skeletons to the southeast. You point the octopod in that direction, and they reward you with a hearty two-tentacle hug. Gain 2 morale. Your act of kindness inspires the Traveler to offer you some advice. “With enough money, you can buy pretty much anything, including a new ship. Maybe visit the casino to the northeast?” Gain 1 boost and destiny 1607[/b] (place it in the center).

261.text: Your impressive feat of memory impresses the sinew sentients, who welcome you to participate in the trial. Continue with the overpower-HUNT[/b][/b] action.

262.text: Your search yields several interesting results. Choose one:

choices.option: Behind the city is the mouth of a cave; enter it
choices.outcome: Go to cave 027[/b].

choices.option: A gateway on the northern end of the city is decorated with a variety of bones; enter the city there.
choices.outcome: Gain 1 engage[/b], go to city 524[/b], and continue with the engage-TRADE[/b][/b] action there.

choices.option: Enter the city through the main gate.
choices.outcome: Go to city 524[/b].

263.text: The odor has a hint of fresh water and vitality to it. Just as you inhale the tempting odor, a hard object smashes you in the head. Then another. Then another. It’s the plant—its bulbous roots are attacking you! Gain 1 boost. Roll 2 skill dice, and gain all overpower[/b] results, then lose 1 health unless you pay 1 overpower[/b]. You may continue.

264.action: SCAN[/b] the ruins for anything useful
264.text: You identify some dusty barrels among the rubble. Gain 1 boost and 1 look[/b]; go to interior 543[/b].

265.text: From what you hear, the lead sentient is a bounty hunter who has captured a hacker from the cold west. You also notice a hulking elemental far in the distance. Gain 1 boost and 1 help[/b]; you may continue.

266.text: Your keen eyes notice several intriguing targets. Choose one:

choices.outcome: The bustling casino seems to be a major attraction, especially in this heat. The employees are inviting anyone and everyone inside. As you get closer, you notice a shiny coin on the ground. Gain $X and 1 boost; you may continue.

choices.option: Smell the lotus to the right
choices.outcome: It smells like coconut. Gain 1 health; you may also lose 1 curse. You may continue.

Each player—including you—on a location with any rune (a skill icon within their vantage) gains 1 boost. Then you roll X skill dice and gain all results matching the rune on this location. Also gain quest 1706[/b] if a player doesn’t already have it (add it horizontally to your supply and place the first token on it).

267.text: You watch the sentient for a few minutes, and it quickly becomes clear that they’re maintaining the oasis in the middle of the desert—perhaps even trying to expand it. Gain 1 boost and 1 help[/b]; you may continue.

268.action: LISTEN[/b] to the Traveler explain the situation
268.text: The Traveler shakes their head. “Historically, the sinew and leaf sentients haven’t gotten along, but in modern times they’ve kept an uneasy peace. This spoiled prince could ruin everything with his repeated attacks, though fortunately most sinew people don’t listen to him.” Gain 2 boost and continue.

269.text: Your keen eyes notice that the funnel, while dangerous and growing in size, isn’t veering from its current position. You may go northwest to 273[/b] or northeast to 447[/b], or continue.

027.text: After close examination, you determine that there is no option other than to modify the laser array, and you calculate the perfect angle to redirect the beams. Gain 1 help[/b] and continue.

270.text: The chest is sturdy (but a little weatherworn), with a solid lock and ornate gold trim. You have a bad feeling about opening it with anything other than near-perfect execution. Gain 2 boost and continue.

271.text: Among the gleaming ivory you find an impressive sword. Gain item 892[/b]; you may continue with any action other than take-SCAVENGE[/b][/b].

272.text: At first the blobs appeared solid to you, but upon closer inspection you realize that they’re constantly twisting, shifting, and changing. Passing sentients keep a safe distance from them, some of them heading to cooler climates to the northeast and northwest. Gain 2 boost and 1 take[/b]; you may continue.

273.text: You find a tight gap in the wreckage that you might be able to squeeze through. It’s surprising that, despite the plummet from outer space, this part of the ship seems operational. Gain 1 boost and destiny 1605[/b] (place it in the center); you may continue.

274.text: The odor has a hint of dry bone. Gain 1 boost and continue.

275.text: The lotus smells a bit like coconut. Gain 1 health from the purifying pollen; you may also lose 1 curse.

276.action: LEARN[/b] how to play the game
276.text: The sentients take a break to explain basic information about the world’s oldest game. They roll a few dice, then each player selects 1 card from their collection for a brief duel against their opponent. Gain ability 1499[/b], and gain either 1 engage[/b] or 1 overpower[/b]; you may continue.

277.text: The beast’s rage is more of a response to its chains than its natural demeanor. While there surely is a way to pass through without harm, you could also free the animal. Gain 1 boost and either 1 move[/b] or 1 help[/b]; you may continue.

278.action: SEARCH[/b] for a gap in the wall
278.text: There are a few dark windows in the wall that could branch into deeper tunnels. As you draw close to one of them, several cloaked figures jump out of it and surround you. Gain 1 boost and read entry 278[/b] in the Book of Secrets.

279.text: The orb is made of a shimmering, glass-like substance, and there are markings on the outside with which you might be able to interact. You don’t see how to access the interior, but there must be a way inside. Gain 1 boost and either 1 engage[/b] or 1 take[/b]; you may continue.

028.text: After close examination, you determine that there is no option other than to befriend the sentinel, and you calculate the perfect conversation starter. Gain 1 engage[/b] and continue.

280.text: The sentient advises you to explore the desert to the east if you’re looking for a crystal that will improve your ability to help and create. You sense there is something else they want to say, but they trail off. Gain 1 boost and continue.

281.text: The main entrance to the building—which appears to be the only entrance—is wide open, and the campfire indicates to you that someone is already inside. You arm yourself with a torch from the fire before proceeding. Gain item 1087[/b] and 1 boost; you may continue.

282.text: It looks like the beast died from thirst. There is a stillness to the air around the skeleton, and you can’t decide whether to use the bones to your advantage or delve underneath. Gain 2 boost and continue.

283.text: Each player—including you—on a location with any rune (a skill icon within their vantage) gains 1 boost. Then you roll X skill dice and gain all results matching the rune on this location. Also gain quest 1706[/b] if a player doesn’t already have it (add it horizontally to your supply and place the first token on it).

284.text: As you prepare to extract the crystal, you perceptively notice the three treasure chests conspicuously at the base of the rock. Why so many chests all in one place? Choose one: Gain 1 random skill and 1 skill of your choice, refresh all challenge dice from a row of your grid, or gain 1 boost on each card in a column of your grid. Continue.

285.text: You find a tight gap in the wreckage that you might be able to squeeze through. It’s surprising that, despite the plummet from outer space, this part of the ship seems operational. Gain 1 boost and destiny 1605[/b] (place it in the center); you may continue.

286.text: Your eyes don’t fool you—these really are three separate sentients merged into one. They don’t seem to notice you or care about their valuables, as they’re busy bickering among themselves. Gain 1 boost and 1 take[/b]; you may continue.

287.action: NAVIGATE[/b] a route to the peak
287.text: It is a treacherous ascent, but you find a well-worn route leading to a sprawling room inside the mountain. Go to interior 592[/b] and gain 1 morale.

288.text: The tiny creature seems incredibly nervous. Whenever it starts to move to your left, they look up at you expectantly. Gain 1 boost and 1 move[/b]; you may continue.

289.text: The outcropping provides a nice foundation for you to build a bridge, though it may be easier simply to get the attention of the passing balloon. Gain 2 boost and continue.

029.text: After close examination, you determine that there is no option other than to modify the laser array, and you calculate the perfect angle to redirect the beams. Gain 2 help[/b] and continue.

290.text: It looks like the beast was eaten by a sand centipede. There is a stillness to the air around the skeleton, and you can’t decide whether to use the bones to your advantage or delve underneath. Gain 2 boost and continue.

291.text: Even in the dark, you can sense small tremors of movement in the sand near the spiky protrusions. You might be safest moving somewhere other than north. Gain 1 boost and 1 move[/b]; you may continue.

292.action: EXPLORE[/b] the mouth of the cave
292.text: As your keen eyes adjust to the darkness, you reach a barrier that requires further attention. Go to cave 025[/b].

293.text: Each player—including you—on a location with any rune (a skill icon within their vantage) gains 1 boost. Then you roll X skill dice and gain all results matching the rune on this location. Also gain quest 1706[/b] if a player doesn’t already have it (add it horizontally to your supply and place the first token on it).

294.action: SPY[/b] through an open seam in the bigger tent, waiting for its occupants to get some fresh air
294.text: Your eyes light up when you see some climbing gear inside the tent. Gain 1 boost and 1 move[/b]; go to interior 552[/b].

295.text: The sentient gestures for you to join them. As they work, they explain the basics of creating and manipulating glass. They guide you through the process of making a glass trinket (gain item 1091[/b]).

296.text: You feel a surge of wisdom as you study the stone and the rune etched into it. For a brief moment you see a vision from the past—this area was the scene of a truce between the sand sentients to the south and gold sentients to the north, where it looks a bit warmer. Gain 1 boost.

297.action: LISTEN[/b] to the silence of the expanse
297.text: As soon as you become fully aware of the quiet, you realize that the desert isn’t entirely silent. A muffled roar emerges from deep underground. Gain 1 boost and continue.

298.text: You feel a surge of wisdom as you study the stones and the rune etched into them. For a brief moment you see a vision from the distant past—this area was a meeting place between friendly gold, metal, and energy sentients. Their discussion focused on the area to the east that would one day host the towers you now see there, as well as their concerns about some corrupted runecasters that were causing some issues to the southeast. The vision also indicates warmer temperatures to the north. Gain 2 boost.

299.text: The tower has a number of good footholds and handholds; you could get a great view of the surrounding area if you scaled it or use the tower’s transmission capabilities to contact your homeworld. As you study it, one of the lights on the tower changes to a pink hue, and a head begins to emerge from it. Gain 1 boost and any 1 skill. Continue.

030.text: After close examination, you determine that there is no option other than to befriend the sentinel, and you calculate the perfect conversation starter. Gain 1 engage[/b] and continue.

300.text: It’s hard to see at first, but when the beast pauses for breath, you can see a large thorn irritating its eye. It may be difficult to heal the animal without first calming or subduing it. Gain 1 boost and either 1 take[/b] or 1 overpower[/b]; you may continue.

301.text: Ducking behind a rock, you scan the valley. In the middle of the circle, a blue sentient is tied to a rock. Pink energy flows from them into the surrounding sentients; some of it even reaches you. Soon the sentient grows limp, and the ceremony ends. Gain ability 1483[/b], 1 morale, and 1 boost.

302.action: LISTEN[/b] to the Traveler talk about the birds
302.text: “These birds are almost biologically identical to those found on your world, and they’re trained to deliver messages. In many places you can simply relax to birdsong, and they might even inspire you to send a signal to your home planet using a tower.” Gain 2 boost and destiny 1598[/b] (place it in the center).

303.text: As you trace the lines on the stone, three distinctly colored icons emerge. You may choose to focus on one of the three. If you choose the green icon, gain spell 1236[/b]. If you choose the pink icon, gain spell 1237[/b]. If you choose the blue icon, gain spell 1238[/b].

304.text: A one-eyed guard stands on the edge of the cliff, anxiously watching the approaching pirate ship. Gain 1 boost and 1 move[/b]; you may continue.

305.text: As far as you can tell, the lighthouse is currently unoccupied. You hear a sentient humming to themselves to the west; perhaps that’s the lighthouse keeper. The door is locked, but the hinge looks weak. Gain 2 boost and 1 overpower[/b]; you may continue.

306.text: You perceptively notice that the 5 nearest floating islands are all positioned in clear view of the shard. Choose one: Gain 1 random skill and 1 skill of your choice, refresh all challenge dice from a row of your grid, or gain 1 boost on each card in a column of your grid. Continue.

307.text: It looks like the goliath died from loneliness. There is a stillness to the air around the skeleton, and you can’t decide whether to use the bones to your advantage or delve underneath. Gain 2 boost and continue.

308.action: INSPECT[/b] either the animal or the orb

choices.outcome: You wade around the animal, looking it up and down for the source of its pain. You find it—a large fishhook has left a long gash in its back and is still wedged there. Gain 1 boost and either 1 help[/b] or 1 overpower[/b]; you may continue with the help-HEAL[/b][/b] or overpower-RELIEVE[/b][/b] action.

The orb is made of a shimmering, glass-like substance, and there are markings on the outside with which you might be able to interact. You don’t see how to access the interior, but there must be a way inside. Gain 1 boost and either 1 engage[/b] or 1 take[/b]; you may continue.

309.action: LISTEN[/b] to the Traveler talk about your mission
309.text: “If you’re on a treasure hunt, there may be no better partners than these pirates.” Gain 1 boost and 1 engage[/b]; you may continue.

031.text: After close examination, you determine that there is no option other than to befriend the sentinel, and you calculate the perfect conversation starter. Gain 1 engage[/b] and continue.

310.text: When you look closely at the rainbow, you notice that it’s more than just a refraction of light—it’s composed of crystal dust. Gain ability 1492[/b], 1 morale, and 1 boost; you may continue.

311.text: You find that the path leads underwater by design. You follow the steps until you’re fully submerged. Choose one:

choices.option: Suffer 1 hypoxia damage to proceed even deeper
choices.outcome: After inhaling too much water, you emerge dripping wet in an underground cave. Go to cave 089[/b].
Gain 1 boost and continue.

312.text: The footprints in the sand suggest that the owner of the boat isn’t far from here, so you could either catch their attention or try to slip away in the boat before they notice. Gain 1 boost and continue.

313.text: You get the feeling that the animal is watching you with just as much curiosity as with which you’re watching it. Perhaps it’s your imagination, but you think you see tiny flames atop the bird’s crest. Gain 1 boost and 1 random skill; you may continue.

314.text: The owner of the boat is nowhere to be seen, but you hear a voice inside the nearby cave. Either take the boat (gain vehicle 1014[/b]) or venture into the cave (go to cave 037[/b]).

315.action: TRANSLATE[/b] a few pages of the book
315.text: Look at page 26 of the Book of Vantages to learn about flora; gain 1 boost and continue.

316.text: Even up close, the animal is nearly indistinguishable from a large mushroom, albeit one that can walk and make sounds. It seems to have a friendly disposition. Gain 1 boost and 1 engage[/b]; you may continue.

317.text: The terrified animal stumbles forward and blends into the trees near the lotus. As you watch, another animal—a giant lizard—emerges from the brush and is immediately entranced by the sweet-smelling lotus. Gain animal 851[/b].

318.text: Set aside 6 cubes representing water. Roll exactly 10 skill dice and randomly arrange 9 of them into a 3x3 grid. Use the extra die to push a row or column so that the extra die enters the grid; a new extra die will emerge from the other end. When it does, you may reroll the new extra die once by spending a water cube. Proceed to push the extra die into the grid, repeating the process. The goal is for each row or column to have 3 identical icons (e.g., the first row has 3 orange icons, the second row has 3 green icons, and the third row has 3 blue icons). Succeed if you accomplish this goal; gain item 1008[/b] and perform no other location actions here. If you spend all water tokens and can’t reroll to make the goal possible, you fail and lose 1 morale.

319.text: The creature seems to have no issue with you watching it work. It’s using the rocks as a canvas for a gold fluid secreted by its claws. You can’t make any sense of it, and this could be an easy opportunity to capture or even kill the animal. Gain 1 boost and either 1 take[/b] or 1 overpower[/b]; you may continue.

032.text: After close examination, you determine that there is no option other than to destroy the gate, and you calculate the perfect angle for an attempt. Gain 1 overpower[/b] and continue.

320.text: The tower has a number of good footholds and handholds; you could get a great view of the surrounding area if you scaled it or use the tower’s transmission capabilities to contact your homeworld. As you study it, one of the lights on the tower changes to a pink hue, and a head begins to emerge from it. Gain 1 boost and any 1 skill. Continue.

321.text: The sentient is distracted by an onrush of young sentients; they say that they’ll teach you soon if you get in line. Go to interior 600[/b].

322.text: The eager sentient explains the meaning of the various positions of petals in their flower arrangement, for which they used the tropical arecaceae flora. Gain ability 1465[/b].

323.action: LEARN[/b] along with the other students
323.text: It takes you a few minutes to catch up, but fortunately this is a beginner class. The instructor is explaining the sacrifice required to tap into life mana. Gain lesson 1419[/b].

324.text: Through the swirling energy that forms the animal’s body, you see the glimmer of a bright orange crystal and an angry flare around a metal spike in its leg. Gain 1 boost and 1 help[/b]; you may continue.

325.text: The netting is similar to a spider web’s, but the strands are thick as a rope and razor-sharp. Gain 1 boost and 1 help[/b]; you may continue.

326.text: The arachnoid creature waits patiently for unwitting prey… or a reason to defend itself. You could placate it if you provide a meal for it instead of serving yourself as the meal. Gain 1 boost and 1 help[/b]; you may continue.

327.text: It’s a sturdy treasure chest featuring a solid lock and ornate gold trim. Concerning, though, is the alarm embedded in its base. Gain 2 boost and continue.

328.text: The sentient refuses to let you pass. Instead, they recommend that you take a whiff of the nearby lotus and then be on your way. Gain 1 boost; you may also lose 1 curse.

329.text: In the hope of finding a way to untether their people from this planet, the sentient sought the legendary elder dragon, but they failed to find their mark. Gain quest 1670[/b] (add it horizontally to your supply).

033.text: After close examination, you determine that there is no option other than to leap, and you calculate the perfect angle for an attempt. Gain 1 move[/b] and continue.

330.text: Each player—including you—on a location with any rune (a skill icon within their vantage) gains 1 boost. Then you roll X skill dice and gain all results matching the rune on this location. Also gain quest 1706[/b] if a player doesn’t already have it (add it horizontally to your supply and place the first token on it).

331.text: The tree is oddly odorless, which would indicate that its composition is pure gold. You’ve never seen anything like it, and you suspect this tree is sacred to those who live in the area. Gain 1 boost and continue.

332.text: With the wind pressing you against the village walls, you find a hidden door. Inside you see a dark hallway capped by a door with a band of light at eye level. If you enter, gain 2 take[/b], go to city 530[/b], and continue with the take-ACCESS[/b][/b] action there. Otherwise, go to city 530[/b].

333.action: SCAN[/b] the inside of the building
333.text: There’s an elaborate welding device nearby that you could probably grab if the store owner were distracted. It’s just you and them on this quiet plateau—no one would know if you rob or even accost the owner. Gain 2 boost and continue.

334.text: The sentient’s biological matter seems to be the gold fluid itself, the outer husk a vessel or exoskeleton. They are in dire need of help. Gain 1 boost and 1 help[/b]; you may continue.

335.text: It looks like the peddler—who is conveniently absent—specializes in equipment for explorers, as you see a compass and a climbing axe among the supplies there. Gain 2 boost and continue.

336.text: This caravan is a merchant procession, and their main feature seems to be large husks. Gain 1 boost and continue.

337.text: Based on the impenetrable armor and lack of trust from the driver, you will be hard-pressed to hitch a ride or to raid the vehicle. You spot a weakness, however, in the “belly” of the transport that you could use to steal a few coins. Gain 2 boost and continue.

338.text: This playground for the rich isn’t the type of place you can just walk into (figuratively or literally). Wealthy sentients lounge about while they’re served by an overly attentive staff. You overhear one of the guests mention suspiciously that they hope the staff won’t steal the ring they left in their room. Gain 2 boost and 1 help[/b]; you may continue.

339.text: The sentients invite you to join their boisterous game. You will try to create the best poker-style hand of dice compared to 2 competing sentients. For this game, roll 2 skill dice for yourself, set aside 4 skill dice for the sentients (2 dice each; you don’t know their results yet), and reserve 3 shared skill dice. Roll 1 shared die. With that knowledge, you may reroll one or both of your dice simultaneously. Then roll the second shared die. Again, you may reroll one or both of your dice. One final time, roll the third shared die, then you may reroll one or both of your dice. Now roll both of the other gamblers’ dice to see their final results (two sets of 2 dice each). The order of possible results from worst to best are one of a kind, one pair, two pairs, three of a kind, full house, four of a kind, and five of a kind. See entry 339[/b] in the Book of Secrets.

034.text: After close examination, you determine that there is no option other than to befriend the sentinel, and you calculate the perfect conversation starter. Gain 1 engage[/b] and continue.

340.text: The owner of the boat isn’t far from here, so you could either catch their attention or try to slip away in the boat before they notice. Gain 1 boost and either 1 help[/b] or 1 overpower[/b]; you may continue.

341.text: Your impressive feat of memory impresses the gold sentients, who welcome you to participate in the trial. Continue with the engage-TRADE[/b][/b] action.

342.text: While looking through the brush, you find a hidden tunnel leading directly into the city embassy. If you enter, gain 2 help[/b], go to city 529[/b], and continue with the help-OFFER[/b][/b] action there. Otherwise, go to city 529[/b].

343.text: The structures to the north appear to be vacant, perhaps deserted in lieu of the newer city to the west. Just because this society decided not to live here any longer doesn’t mean you couldn’t build something on the river bank. Gain 1 boost and 1 help[/b]; you may continue.

344.action: STUDY[/b] the city from various angles
344.text: Your suspicions are confirmed—the “city” in the distance is a well-crafted mirage. When you point this out to a local, they offer you a mischievous glance. Depending on who you ask, they say, the illusion was either created to confuse invaders or to make the land of the gold sentients appear more opulent than it really is. Impressed at your perception, the sentient teaches you a spell-related lesson. Gain lesson 1426[/b].

345.text: The animal’s big eyes blink in fear. Your choice to watch instead of act is a death sentence for the animal. Lose 1 morale and gain 1 boost.

346.text: There is someone moving around on the top floor. The building has the feel of a fortress, but that doesn’t explain the sparkling wind. Gain 1 boost and 1 move[/b]; you may continue.

347.text: The lotus smells a bit like coconut. Gain 1 health from the purifying pollen; you may also lose 1 curse.

348.text: Each player—including you—on a location with any rune (a skill icon within their vantage) gains 1 boost. Then you roll X skill dice and gain all results matching the rune on this location. Also gain quest 1706[/b] if a player doesn’t already have it (add it horizontally to your supply and place the first token on it).

349.text: The sounds of civilization trickle in from the south, while the smell of healthier flowers wafts from the north. You can sense that the Traveler is willing to share information with you, and you might take a closer look at the nearby tower—it looks like it could serve several purposes. Gain 1 boost and 1 random skill; you may continue.

035.text: After close examination, you determine that there is no option other than to swap out the jelly-like substance with something of similar weight, and you scour the cave entrance for the perfect object. Gain 1 take[/b] and continue.

350.action: EXPLORE[/b] the mouth of the cave
350.text: As your keen eyes adjust to the darkness, you reach a barrier that requires further attention. Go to cave 029[/b].

351.text: The tower has a number of good footholds and handholds; you could get a great view of the surrounding area if you scaled it. You also find yourself focusing on the purple rune on the bank of the river. Each player—including you—on a location with any rune (a skill icon within their vantage) gains 1 boost. Then you roll X skill dice and gain all results matching the rune on this location. Also gain quest 1706[/b] if a player doesn’t already have it (add it horizontally to your supply and place the first token on it).

352.text: The sentient’s teaching skills are rough around the edges, but you pick up the core concepts by watching them. Gain ability 1461[/b] and 1 health; you may continue.

353.text: The air gets colder as you approach the tower, which is attempting to manipulate the mild climate through a combination of technology and something you can best describe as magic. Your close examination results in some of that magic merging with you. Gain spell 1377[/b].

354.text: The odor reminds you of formaldehyde, which makes you squirm a bit. You’re glad no one is around to see you. Gain 1 boost and continue.

355.action: SEARCH[/b] the wreckage and surrounding area
355.text: You find a tight gap in the wreckage that you might be able to squeeze through. It’s surprising that, despite the plummet from outer space, this part of the ship seems operational. Gain 2 boost. You also find a sweet-smelling lotus nearby. Gain 1 health; you may also lose 1 curse. You may continue.

356.action: OBSERVE[/b] the disturbance in the water
356.text: As you watch, a massive tongue flicks out of the spiky circle and grabs a bird out of the air. You might be wise to distract the underwater creature before moving north. Gain 1 boost and 1 take[/b]; you may continue.

357.text: As you watch what looks at first like an act of destruction, you realize that the sentients inside the house seem to appreciate the fire—they’re basking in it. Gain 1 boost and 2 engage[/b]; you may continue.

358.text: The sentient nods, then continues their conversation with the Traveler. They speak of the embarrassment of the sinew elemental to the southwest and that they would offer riches to anyone who freed the elemental. Gain quest 1633[/b] (add it horizontally to your supply).

359.text: The sentient’s attention remains on the fishing rod, but they talk slowly about various methods of catching fish (gain ability 1461[/b]).

036.text: The barrier is a pitch-black, endless void. Finally you emerge much deeper into the planet; go to cave 096[/b].

360.text: Each player—including you—on a location with any rune (a skill icon within their vantage) gains 1 boost. Then you roll X skill dice and gain all results matching the rune on this location. Also gain quest 1706[/b] if a player doesn’t already have it (add it horizontally to your supply and place the first token on it).

361.text: Each player—including you—on a location with any rune (a skill icon within their vantage) gains 1 boost. Then you roll X skill dice and gain all results matching the rune on this location. Also gain quest 1706[/b] if a player doesn’t already have it (add it horizontally to your supply and place the first token on it).

362.text: You pull a leaf toward you, and it sticks to your fingers. You pry it loose, considering uses for this adhesive flora. Gain 1 boost and continue.

363.text: For each other player, gain 1 skill matching their character’s leftmost challenge die slot. If you are the only player, gain any 1 skill instead. You may continue.

364.text: Each player—including you—on a location with any rune (a skill icon within their vantage) gains 1 boost. Then you roll X skill dice and gain all results matching the rune on this location. Also gain quest 1706[/b] if a player doesn’t already have it (add it horizontally to your supply and place the first token on it).

365.text: Despite the salty water splashing into your eyes, you notice two important details: Firstly, the water is curiously calm under the surface. Secondly, the lights in the distance seem to be emanating from the same, unmoving position. Gain 1 boost and 1 random skill; you may continue.

366.text: While fish nibble at the bait, a storm brews to the west. A treasure chest bobs on the tide to the east. If you want to go north, you’ll need to be able to hold your breath. Gain any 3 skills and continue.

367.text: The chest is sturdy (but a little weatherworn), with a solid lock and ornate gold trim. Gain 2 boost and continue.

368.text: Each player—including you—on a location with any rune (a skill icon within their vantage) gains 1 boost. Then you roll X skill dice and gain all results matching the rune on this location. Also gain quest 1706[/b] if a player doesn’t already have it (add it horizontally to your supply and place the first token on it).

369.action: PREPARE[/b] to interact with the elemental
369.text: Choose one: Gain 1 random skill and 1 skill of your choice, refresh all challenge dice from a row of your grid, or gain 1 boost on each card in a column of your grid. Then continue with either the help-RELEASE[/b][/b] action or the overpower-SLAY[/b][/b] action.

037.text: After close examination, you determine that there is no option other than to destroy the gate, and you calculate the perfect angle for an attempt. Gain 1 overpower[/b] and continue.

370.text: The waters to the east are protected by metal rods, and there’s a treasure chest to the south that intrigues you. You can’t look away, however, from the creepy elemental to the west. Gain 2 boost and continue.

371.text: The flower has absolutely no aroma at all. Gain 1 boost and continue.

372.text: Based on the pattern on the far shore, the safe path clearly appears to be the green lily pads. You’re unsure of the dangers that lurk beneath the surface, but it’s bad enough that several shock rods to the west keep it contained here. Gain 1 boost and 1 move[/b]; you may continue.

373.text: The creature is similar in size to the larger dinosaurs of Old Earth. This beast’s anatomy is unique in that it seems to have an entire ecosystem of flora growing from its leafy scales. Gain 1 boost and either 1 engage[/b] or 1 help[/b]; you may continue.

374.text: The animal seems protective of the barrels half-buried along the shoreline, so you stay clear of them. Seeing your effort, the animal’s posture and tone shift to friendly, even chatty. Gain animal 838[/b].

375.text: The owners of the boat wave at you and signal to avoid trouble to the south. Gain 1 boost and continue.

376.text: A lone sentient is inside the building, occasionally warming a large egg with a blowtorch. Gain 2 boost and 1 random skill; you may continue.

377.text: The odor smells a bit like a charcoal grill. The sentients to the east heckle you and ask why you’re smelling such a scalding hot flower. Gain 1 boost and continue.

378.action: LISTEN[/b] to the sentients hawk their wares
378.text: Each merchant specializes in a different type of item. There are vendors for crystals, tools, weapons, garments, maps, and even flora. Their prices seem reasonable, and they’re constantly shouting over each other. Gain 1 engage[/b] and continue.

379.text: The lotus smells a bit like coconut. Gain 1 health from the purifying pollen; you may also lose 1 curse.

038.text: After close examination, you determine that there is no option other than to swap out the stones with something of similar weight, and you scour the cave entrance for the perfect object. Gain 1 take[/b] and continue.

380.text: The animal is in great pain, to the point that it can barely move. A closer examination reveals something wedged under its shell, putting pressure on the animal’s spine. Gain 2 help[/b] and continue.

381.text: The odd vessel appears to be unowned and fully autonomous. The driver steers the large turtle from inside the bulbous cabin, which can carry a landwalker like you underwater or an ocean dweller onto land. Gain 2 boost and continue.

382.action: SEARCH[/b] the surrounding area for the source of the gash
382.text: You had a hunch that the cause of the broken boulder—which is still in the process of mending itself—is nearby, and that hunch was correct. In the bushes you find a mysterious sword. Gain item 889[/b].

383.text: Your approach leaves the sentient hesitant, but once they start talking, you get the impression that they haven’t had anyone to converse with in a long time. While sharing a snack with you, they explain that they run the nearby lighthouse, a job bestowed upon them due to their excellent vision. They’ve recently noticed a curious box of pure light to the north, and they ask you kindly to investigate it. Gain 1 health and quest 1672[/b] (add it horizontally to your supply).

384.text: The chest is sturdy (but a little moss-worn), with a solid lock and ornate gold trim. Gain 2 boost and continue.

385.action: SCAN[/b] the ruins for anything useful
385.text: You identify a hammock among the rubble. Gain 1 boost and 1 overpower[/b]; go to interior 544[/b].

386.text: The lotus smells a bit like coconut. Gain 1 health from the purifying pollen; you may also lose 1 curse.

387.text: You can’t picture a better place to build a farm. There are sentients in the area you could hire to help, or you could do it yourself. Gain 2 boost and continue.

388.text: The rushing water churns the mud, turning the river a dark brown—swimming north against the current will prove difficult. There’s a long bridge leading into the city to the west, but it’s heavily guarded by sentients collecting tolls from arriving travelers. Gain 2 boost and continue.

389.text: The odor has a zest to it, giving you a surge of adrenaline. Gain 1 health and 1 boost; you may continue.

039.text: The barrier is a pitch-black, endless void. Finally you emerge much deeper into the planet; go to cave 099[/b].

390.text: Among the berries you find a small hole in the wall. You may wiggle through it to go west to 787[/b].

391.text: The wall here seems lower than other places; you could probably scale it. The ground is soft from a recent rainfall, so you could even dig under the wall. Gain 2 boost, and gain either 1 move[/b] or 1 overpower[/b]; you may continue.

392.text: With the sounds of a large city carrying over the wall, you can see what appears to be some sort of metallic beast in the distance. To the east are several massive animals in flight. Gain 2 boost and continue.

393.action: PREPARE[/b] to interact with the elemental
